description_long: This guard stone consists of a robust, rectangular stone base with two thick, upright pillars at each end, capped with slightly protruding tops. Between the pillars, a curved stone beam supports a row of five evenly spaced, upward-pointing metal spikes, each with a sharp arrowhead tip. The stone appears weathered and dark, with a rough, matte finish, suggesting exposure to the elements over time. The metal spikes are black and slightly irregular, possibly forged iron, and show signs of minor bending or warping. The overall construction is solid and heavy, designed to act as a formidable barrier against vehicles or to demarcate boundaries. The object exudes a sense of durability and deterrence, suitable for protecting corners or entrances in urban environments.
description: A heavy stone guard barrier with two upright pillars and a curved beam supporting five sharp metal spikes. The weathered stone and black iron spikes create a formidable, durable obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the stone base, both pillars, and all five metal spikes clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the stone structure and the profile of one pillar, with spikes seen edge-on.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the stone base, pillars, and the alignment of the spikes from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the stone's depth and the side profile of the spikes and pillar.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the edge of urban streets, near the corners of buildings, at driveway entrances, or along the perimeter of parks to prevent vehicle encroachment and protect pedestrians.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be fully above ground, with the base resting flush on a paved or concrete surface and the spikes pointing upward.
attribute_wear_patterns: Visible surface wear includes chipping and erosion along the stone edges, likely from vehicle contact and weathering. The tops of the pillars and the area around the spikes show smoothing and discoloration from repeated exposure to rain and pollution. The metal spikes exhibit minor rust and bending, especially at the tips, due to impact and environmental exposure.
